course_title: KINEMATICS, DYNAMICS, AND CONTROL OF ROBOTIC MANIPULATORS
description: Robotics analysis and design, focusing on the analytical fundamentals specific to robotic manipulators.  Serial chain robotic manipulator forward and inverse kinematics, differential kinematics, dynamics, trajectory generation, and controls. Builds on knowledge of high-level computational programming language such as Matlab.

prerequisites.prerequisites_text: M E 340and (MATH 320,340,341, or375), graduate/professional standing, or member of Engineering Guest Students. Not open to students with credit for E C E 739 prior to fall 2024.
